cycleTopics: Review whether plan.extension.json is necessary

plan.topic: Review agent-tracker lifecycle and purpose

issues.title: Decide the intended operating model for agent-tracker.json and whether current reset behavior is a bug
issues.decision: `agent-tracker.json` is a session-scoped runtime continuity/observability state, not durable invocation history. Reset behavior in `ensureNexusStructure()` is incorrect and should be removed. Tracker reset must occur only at explicit session lifecycle boundaries, while normal setup/init/sync paths should preserve the current tracker. Current consumers should continue treating tracker as ephemeral runtime state layered on top of canonical plan/task files.

plan.research_summary: Current opencode-nexus code uses `.nexus/state/opencode-nexus/agent-tracker.json` as runtime continuity/observability state, not canonical history. `src/shared/agent-tracker.ts` itself only appends or updates invocations by `invocation_id`; it does not delete entries. The observed 'entries appear and disappear' behavior comes from whole-file resets: `ensureNexusStructure()` resets the tracker (`src/shared/state.ts:14-27`), and that function is called from session initialization and also workflow/setup paths. `session.created` also calls `resetAgentTracker()` again directly (`src/plugin/hooks.ts:119-123`), so tracker resets twice on session start. Current consumers use tracker for plan follow-up continuity, context summaries, and active-agent snapshots, which fits an ephemeral runtime-state role better than durable history. The main problem is a lifecycle/design mismatch: the file looks persistent, but implementation resets it from overly broad code paths.

tasks.title: Stop destructive tracker resets from ensure/setup paths
tasks.context: Plan #38 decided that agent-tracker.json is session-scoped runtime state and must not be reset from generic ensure/setup/init/sync paths. Current code resets tracker inside ensureNexusStructure() and again on session.created, causing invocations to appear and disappear unexpectedly.
tasks.approach: Refactor structure/setup helpers so they only ensure directories/files exist. Keep tracker reset only at explicit session lifecycle boundaries in hooks. Preserve runtime behavior otherwise.
tasks.acceptance: ensureNexusStructure() is non-destructive; nx_init/nx_sync/nx_setup no longer wipe agent-tracker state; session.created remains the only runtime reset path unless another explicit lifecycle hook is intentionally added.
tasks.risk: Changing initialization boundaries can expose assumptions in tests or startup behavior.

tasks.title: Align tests and docs with agent-tracker lifecycle model
tasks.context: After lifecycle behavior is corrected, tests and docs should describe agent-tracker as session-scoped runtime continuity state and stop implying it is durable invocation history or reset by generic ensure/setup flows.
tasks.approach: Update targeted tests plus human/generated docs that describe agent-tracker semantics, reset boundaries, or lifecycle expectations.
tasks.acceptance: Relevant tests pass with the new lifecycle, and active docs describe agent-tracker as ephemeral runtime continuity/observability state reset at session boundaries only.
tasks.risk: Docs/generated drift if source-of-truth files are not updated first.

tasks.title: Verify agent-tracker lifecycle end-to-end
tasks.context: After lifecycle and docs/tests are updated, verify that tracker state persists across non-session operations, resets only at session boundaries, and still supports continuity/context features.
tasks.approach: Run targeted lifecycle/continuity tests and broader checks. Review for remaining reset paths or stale contract statements.
tasks.acceptance: Tracker no longer clears during non-session setup/init/sync paths, session-start reset behavior remains explicit, and relevant tests/checks pass.
tasks.risk: Hidden code paths may still reset the tracker or rely on old semantics.

cycleTopics: Review agent-tracker lifecycle and purpose

tasks.title: Sync .nexus/context docs with current repository state
tasks.context: User requested [sync]. Recent changes affect skill metadata, runtime dependencies, and E2E/build workflow details in .nexus/context.
tasks.approach: Compare changed source files and package metadata against current .nexus/context docs, then apply targeted documentation updates only where concrete drift exists.
tasks.acceptance: Context docs accurately reflect current skill triggers, dependencies, tool/runtime facts, and E2E workflow/script inventory without rewriting unrelated sections.
tasks.risk: Over-updating abstract context based on weak signals; avoid speculative edits and leave unchanged sections intact.

tasks.title: Track agent-tracker entries from initial subagent delegation
tasks.context: User noticed agent-tracker entries appear only after subagent completion and asked to manage status from the first call under [run].
tasks.approach: Adjust agent-tracker lifecycle schema and start registration so an invocation is appended immediately with a non-terminal state, then keep terminal transition on completion. Update affected readers/tests accordingly.
tasks.acceptance: agent-tracker appends an invocation record before subagent completion with an explicit pre-completion status, existing completion logic still works, and relevant tests cover the lifecycle.
tasks.risk: Changing lifecycle enum could break running-status filters; keep semantics narrow and update only affected comparisons.

tasks.title: Align opencode tracker lifecycle to running-completed
tasks.context: User requested [run] and wants opencode-nexus agent-tracker lifecycle to match claude-nexus: no pending state, only running -> completed. Current recent change introduced pending as the initial status.
tasks.approach: Revert the pending lifecycle change narrowly: remove pending from schema/status helpers, restore applyInvocationStart to running, update active checks and the focused E2E expectation accordingly, then run build plus focused tracker test.
tasks.acceptance: Agent tracker schema no longer includes pending, invocation start records running immediately, active logic remains correct, and build plus e2e-agent-tracker-core pass.
tasks.risk: Leaving any pending-aware comparisons behind could create dead branches or mismatched active-state handling.

plan.topic: Diagnose stale task-pipeline prompting and unauthorized task_close behavior

issues.title: Why non-run flows still get task-pipeline prompting and guidance
issues.decision: Adopt claude-nexus-style run scoping for task pipeline guidance. Remove full task-pipeline instructions from global prompt layers and make detailed nx_task_add/nx_task_update/nx_task_close procedure run-mode-only. Specifically: system prompt should not always inject the TASK PIPELINE block or global 'enforce task pipeline for code changes' language; the nexus primary prompt should scope task-pipeline instructions to [run]; quick-start/onboarding text should stop advertising nx_task_close as a general step; and non-run/no-tag notices should not instruct task-pipeline actions, only optionally mention that a run cycle is open. Keep hard runtime enforcement state-driven via actual task-cycle files, but stop globally prompting task pipeline outside [run].

issues.title: Why subagents can invoke nx_task_close even though only Nexus should close cycles
issues.decision: Enforce nx_task_close as Nexus-only at both policy and runtime layers. Subagents must not be able to see or call nx_task_close as part of their normal tool affordance, and any attempted invocation must still be rejected server-side unless the caller is the Nexus lead. Concretely: add nx_task_close to subagent-disallowed tool policy, update subagent guidance to state that cycle closure is lead-owned, and add a hard runtime guard in the task-close execution path or pre-execution hook that rejects non-Nexus callers regardless of prompt/tool configuration drift.

issues.title: Why architect subagent invocation did not appear in agent-tracker
issues.decision: Adopt a shared delegation-tracking registrar so agent-tracker covers both OpenCode-internal task-tool subagent spawns and lead-mediated external delegation. Do not treat the OpenCode task hook as the single source of truth, because OpenCode's plugin hook contract only observes in-runtime tool execution and cannot see external delegation paths. Instead, define agent-tracker's single logical source as a common tracking layer used by both paths: internal task-hook instrumentation will continue to record OpenCode task-based spawns, and external delegation entrypoints must call the same registrar for start/end lifecycle updates. Preserve invocation correlation semantics across both sources so the tracker remains a unified observability surface for all Nexus-mediated subagent work.

issues.title: Why plan-mode option discussions were not consistently presented as comparison tables
issues.decision: Handle plan-format drift with both operator discipline and consumer-side reminder reinforcement, without editing the canonical nexus-core nx-plan prompt body. Treat the immediate cause as lead response discipline drift: in interactive plan sessions, option discussions must start with a comparison table rather than prose. Reinforce this at the opencode-nexus consumer layer, following the claude-nexus pattern, by adding or strengthening plan-state reminder text that explicitly says to present a comparison table with pros/cons/trade-offs/recommendation before asking for a decision. Preserve nexus-core as the canonical source for the skill body; use harness-specific notices to improve compliance rather than forking the prompt content.

plan.research_summary: Researched prompt injection and task workflow enforcement paths. Found task-pipeline instructions are still injected globally outside run mode from multiple places: src/plugin/system-prompt.ts always appends a TASK PIPELINE block and a global constraint '- Enforce task pipeline for code changes' regardless of mode; src/agents/primary.ts tells the nexus primary 'For execution, use the task pipeline. Register tasks before file edits...' without run-mode scoping; src/plugin/hooks.ts adds onboarding/stateful notices that mention nx_task_close and active task-cycle resumption even when no [run] tag is present if tasks.json exists. For unauthorized task_close, subagent tool policy does not forbid nx_task_close: create-config.ts only blocks tools listed in AGENT_META.disallowedTools, and generated agent metadata disallows nx_task_add for many subagents but not nx_task_close for any. Generated subagent prompts also only forbid nx_task_add/nx_task_update in some roles, not nx_task_close. Finally, src/tools/task.ts nxTaskClose executes for any caller and hooks.ts only checks cycle state before nx_task_close, not caller identity, so any subagent that sees the tool can invoke it.

tasks.title: Introduce shared delegation-tracking registrar for agent-tracker
tasks.context: Apply plan issue #3 decision. agent-tracker must cover both OpenCode task-tool subagent spawns and lead-mediated external delegation, using one logical tracking layer.
tasks.approach: Refactor tracker registration around a shared registrar API that both internal task-hook instrumentation and external delegation entrypoints can call for start/end updates. Preserve existing correlation semantics where possible and keep tracker as unified observability surface.
tasks.acceptance: There is a shared tracking layer used by internal task-hook path and external delegation path; architect-style external delegation can be recorded in agent-tracker; existing internal task-tool tracking continues to work.
tasks.risk: Correlation semantics may regress if registrar integration is partial or mismatched.

tasks.title: Scope task-pipeline guidance to run mode and strengthen plan comparison-table reminders
tasks.context: Apply plan issue #1 and #4 decisions. Remove global task-pipeline instructions outside run mode, keep run-specific guidance, and add consumer-side plan reminder text for comparison tables without editing canonical nexus-core skill bodies.
tasks.approach: Update global/system/primary prompt assembly and stateful notices so detailed nx_task_add/nx_task_update/nx_task_close guidance appears only in run mode. Keep non-run notices informational. Add explicit plan-state reminder to present comparison tables with pros/cons/trade-offs/recommendation before asking for a decision, mirroring claude-nexus consumer-side reinforcement.
tasks.acceptance: Non-run system/onboarding/no-tag prompts no longer instruct task pipeline actions; run mode still provides detailed pipeline guidance; plan reminders explicitly mention comparison tables; canonical generated nx-plan prompt bodies remain untouched.
tasks.risk: Over-removal could weaken run guidance or break existing tests relying on prompt fragments.

tasks.title: Make nx_task_close Nexus-only in tool policy and runtime
tasks.context: Apply plan issue #2 decision. Subagents must not be able to see or successfully invoke nx_task_close; runtime must reject non-Nexus callers even if policy drifts.
tasks.approach: Add nx_task_close to subagent-disallowed tool policy and update any subagent-facing guidance if needed. Add a hard runtime guard in task close execution or pre-execution hook that only allows the Nexus lead agent/session to close cycles.
tasks.acceptance: Subagent configs no longer expose nx_task_close; attempts by non-Nexus callers are rejected at runtime; Nexus lead can still close cycles successfully.
tasks.risk: Guarding by the wrong identity signal could block legitimate lead closes.

cycleTopics: Diagnose stale task-pipeline prompting and unauthorized task_close behavior

plan.topic: claude-nexus 대비 opencode-nexus의 prompts/hooks/agents/skills 차이 비교 및 선택 논의

issues.title: Prompt and skill source-of-truth and delivery strategy: generated TS + system injection vs generated Markdown/frontmatter + plugin skill directory
issues.decision: OpenCode harness 구조에 의해 generated TS + dual delivery(A-leg native skill install + B-leg system injection) 방식을 유지한다. Markdown/frontmatter 중심 전환은 하지 않는다. 다만 사람이 검토하기 쉬운 read-only mirror artifact 추가는 후속 개선 후보로 남긴다.

issues.title: Hook architecture and orchestration enforcement: distributed OpenCode hooks vs centralized Claude gate
issues.decision: OpenCode에서는 distributed hooks 구조를 유지한다. centralized gate 스타일로 재구성하지 않는다. 현재 우선순위는 사람이 읽기 쉬운 단일 진입점보다 코드 레벨에서의 책임 분리와 구조적 아름다움이다.

issues.title: Agent and skill harness adaptation differences: metadata, tool restrictions, invocation semantics, and knowledge injection
issues.decision: Agent/skill의 harness-local adaptation을 유지한다. Claude와의 메타데이터/행동 표면 정렬보다 OpenCode 런타임 contract에 맞는 로컬 적응을 우선한다.

issues.title: Where opencode-nexus should intentionally align with claude-nexus vs stay harness-specific
issues.decision: 런타임 동작 기준으로는 다음을 채택한다: (1) opencode-nexus의 고도화된 agent-tracker/continuity 모델은 유지, (2) nx_task_close의 Nexus-lead only 정책은 유지, (3) Claude의 SubagentStop 미완료 task 경고 패턴은 opencode에 부분 도입 검토, (4) task pipeline edit gate는 실제 run 사이클에 한정되도록 재검토한다.

issues.title: agent-tracker가 예기치 않게 빈 배열로 reset되는 원인 추적: session.created 범위, subagent session lifecycle, fallback-empty overwrite 가능성
issues.decision: agent-tracker reset scope는 B안으로 결정한다. tracker는 단일 파일 모델을 유지하되, reset은 primary/lead session created에만 한정한다. child/subagent session created가 존재하더라도 전체 tracker를 비우지 않도록 가드한다.

issues.title: tool-log/files_touched attribution 누락 여부 검증: child session edit가 invocation에 귀속되지 않는지 확인하고 수정 옵션 비교
issues.decision: tool-log/files_touched attribution은 B안으로 결정한다. file-edit events를 child session 기준으로 더 넓게 기록하고, task completion 시 child_session_id가 확정되면 그 세션의 과거 edit 로그를 retroactive join하여 agent-tracker invocation.files_touched를 채운다. 실시간 active invocation 매칭에 의존하지 않는다.

issues.title: task pipeline edit gate scope 검증: idle/free edit, run without tasks, completed-open cycle, active run cycle에서 edit-like tool 차단 범위 확인
issues.decision: task pipeline edit gate scope는 B안으로 결정한다. edit-like tool gate는 실제 run-cycle 관리에 한정하고, no tasks file 상태 자체를 edit 금지로 해석하지 않는다. completed-open cycle에 대한 차단은 유지하되, idle/free edit는 허용한다.

issues.title: SubagentStop equivalent warning in OpenCode: tool.execute.after(task)에서 owner task 미완료 경고를 제공할지 결정
issues.decision: SubagentStop unfinished-task warning은 B안으로 결정한다. OpenCode에는 SubagentStop 훅이 없으므로 Claude 구현을 그대로 복제하지 않고, tool.execute.after(task) 시점에 owner task 미완료 여부를 검사해 equivalent warning/escalation을 제공한다.

issues.title: stateful notice를 실제 system prompt 경로에 연결할지 결정
issues.decision: stateful notice는 B안으로 결정한다. buildStatefulNotice()를 dead helper로 두지 않고 experimental.chat.system.transform 경로에 실제 연결하여, 현재 plan/run/task state에 맞는 짧은 동적 notice를 고정 Nexus system prompt 앞에 주입한다.

issues.title: stop/exit enforcement 강화: active cycle과 completed-open cycle에 차등 enforcement를 적용할지 결정
issues.decision: stop/exit enforcement는 D안으로 결정한다. active cycle에서는 exit-like command에 대해 hard block 수준의 강한 enforcement를 적용하고, completed-open cycle에서는 nx_task_close를 유도하는 강한 warning 또는 1회성 soft block을 사용한다. Claude의 Stop 훅을 그대로 복제하지 않고, OpenCode에서는 exit command guard를 강화하는 방향으로 구현한다.

issues.title: plan skill의 Step 7(task generation) 이 실제 운영에서 누락된 원인 분석: allComplete 이후 tasks.json 생성/유도/전이 부족 이유 진단
issues.decision: plan skill의 Step 7(task generation) 누락 대응은 우선 B안으로 결정한다. interactive plan에서 nx_plan_decide 결과가 allComplete=true가 되면, plan completion 상태를 강하게 알리고 즉시 Step 7(task generation via nx_task_add)로 전이하라는 notice/system-level 유도를 추가한다. dedicated task-generation tool은 후속 개선 후보로 남긴다.

plan.research_summary: Compared opencode-nexus and claude-nexus across authoritative runtime surfaces. opencode-nexus uses build-time generated TS prompt catalogs (`src/agents/generated/*`, `src/skills/generated/*`) plus OpenCode hook/system-prompt injection (`src/plugin/hooks.ts`, `src/plugin/system-prompt.ts`) and installs native `.opencode/skills/*` files via `src/create-config.ts`. claude-nexus uses generated Markdown/frontmatter artifacts (`agents/*.md`, `skills/*/SKILL.md`) as runtime prompt assets, exposes skills through `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`, and centralizes runtime interception/sync/tag dispatch in `src/hooks/gate.ts`. Core prompt bodies mostly match because both consume `@moreih29/nexus-core`, but harness-specific transforms differ: Claude adds frontmatter, maxTurns, slash/manual invocation semantics, and harness-content enrichment; OpenCode adds TS metadata objects, tool policies, and `<nexus>` / `<nexus-skill>` system injection. Hook models differ substantially: OpenCode distributes behavior across typed hooks (`event`, `tool.execute.before/after`, `chat.message`, `command.execute.before`, `experimental.chat.system.transform`) with explicit runtime state files; Claude concentrates more orchestration inside `gate.ts` (Stop, PreToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, CLAUDE.md sync, task reminders, tag dispatch, subagent context injection). Both share tag ids and many planning rules, including comparison-table requirement before decisions, but differ in delivery path, trigger mechanism, and runtime constraints. Recent opencode changes also scope task-pipeline enforcement to run mode and make `nx_task_close` Nexus-lead only.

tasks.title: Update architecture.md with delegation tracker abstraction
tasks.context: Updating .nexus/context/ documentation to match recent source changes: delegation tracker now uses createDelegationTrackerRegistrar factory pattern
tasks.approach: Edit shared section to mention createDelegationTrackerRegistrar abstraction; edit hooks section to mention nx_task_close enforcement and plan mode comparison table requirement
tasks.acceptance: architecture.md mentions createDelegationTrackerRegistrar and nx_task_close enforcement in hooks

tasks.title: Update tools.md with nx_task_close Nexus-lead restriction
tasks.context: Updating .nexus/context/ documentation to match recent source changes: nx_task_close is now Nexus-lead only at both hook and tool levels
tasks.approach: Edit nx_task_close description to mention Nexus-lead restriction; add note to nx_plan_decide about comparison table requirement
tasks.acceptance: tools.md nx_task_close entry notes it is Nexus-lead only; plan mode notes comparison table requirement

tasks.title: Agent tracker reset 재현 실험 추가 및 실행
tasks.context: Investigate unexpected empty agent-tracker.json by reproducing lifecycle behavior in a controlled script. Focus on whether session.created resets tracker after subagent activity and distinguish reset from parse-fallback overwrite.
tasks.approach: Add a focused e2e diagnostic script that exercises createHooks with session.created and task lifecycle events, inspects tracker contents before/after each step, and prints evidence. Run the script after building to collect exact observations.
tasks.acceptance: A repeatable script demonstrates tracker behavior with concrete before/after state and supports narrowing the root cause. Findings are summarized back to the user.
tasks.risk: Synthetic event payloads may not perfectly match real OpenCode runtime events, so conclusions may still need one final live validation.

tasks.title: Tool-log/files_touched 귀속 실험
tasks.context: Verify whether child-session file edits are correctly attributed into tool-log.jsonl and aggregated into agent-tracker files_touched during task lifecycle.
tasks.approach: Add a focused diagnostic e2e script that simulates task start, child-session edit before task completion, and task completion, then inspects tool-log.jsonl and agent-tracker files_touched. Use it to determine whether current matching by child_session_id causes attribution loss.
tasks.acceptance: Repeatable evidence shows whether tool-log attribution works or fails, with exact before/after state and code-path-based interpretation.
tasks.risk: Synthetic hook payloads may differ from production payload ordering, but should still validate the current matching logic assumptions.

tasks.title: Task pipeline edit gate scope 실험
tasks.context: Verify whether edit-like tool gating in hooks is limited to active run cycles or also blocks idle/free edits without a task cycle.
tasks.approach: Add a focused e2e script that invokes tool.execute.before for an edit-like tool across four states: no tasks file, empty tasks file, active task cycle, and completed-open cycle. Capture pass/fail behavior and compare with desired run-only gating semantics.
tasks.acceptance: Repeatable evidence shows the exact current gate scope and whether it over-blocks free edits outside run cycles.
tasks.risk: Synthetic hook invocation may not include all runtime prompt context, but tool gating behavior is implemented directly in hook code and should be representative.

tasks.title: Guard agent-tracker reset to primary session lifecycle only
tasks.context: Implement the decided fix for unexpected tracker wipes. The tracker should remain a single shared file, but reset only for primary/lead session.created boundaries, not child/subagent session creation.
tasks.approach: Narrow the session.created reset path in plugin hooks using reliable primary-session discrimination. Preserve current tracker schema and continuity consumers. Extend the tracker reset experiment to cover the guarded case.
tasks.acceptance: agent-tracker.json is not reset by child/subagent session creation; primary session creation still resets as intended; regression script covers both cases.
tasks.risk: OpenCode event payload may not expose a stable primary/child signal, so the implementation may need a conservative fallback or a follow-up live validation.

tasks.title: Retroactively attribute child-session file edits into files_touched
tasks.context: Current tool-log attribution misses both pre-completion and post-completion child edits. Implement the decided retroactive join strategy so files_touched is populated from session-scoped edit history after task completion reveals child_session_id.
tasks.approach: Record file-edit events broadly by session, then on task completion join historical edit entries for the resolved child_session_id back into the matching tracker invocation and files_touched. Replace active-invocation-only matching with session-based reconciliation. Extend attribution experiments accordingly.
tasks.acceptance: child edits performed before task completion are captured in files_touched after completion; session-based retroactive join works deterministically in regression tests.
tasks.risk: Session-scoped logging may capture unrelated edits if child sessions are reused unexpectedly; aggregation must stay narrow and reproducible.

tasks.title: Limit edit gate to real run-cycle management
tasks.context: Current edit gating blocks free edits when tasks.json is absent. Adjust hook enforcement so only real run-cycle states are gated, while preserving completed-open protection.
tasks.approach: Remove task_cycle_required as a hard edit blocker, keep completed-open close-cycle enforcement, and update the evaluator/hook notices so idle or free edits are allowed without tasks.json. Refresh regression scripts for no-tasks, empty-tasks, active, and completed-open states.
tasks.acceptance: no tasks file no longer blocks edit-like tools; active cycle behavior stays intact; completed-open still blocks edits until close or new cycle setup.
tasks.risk: Changing gating semantics may expose assumptions in existing tests or prompt text that treated tasks.json absence as mandatory.

tasks.title: Inject stateful notices and all-complete Step 7 handoff into system guidance
tasks.context: buildStatefulNotice currently exists as a dead helper, and plan completion does not strongly hand off into Step 7 task generation. Wire the dynamic notice path into system transform and strengthen plan-complete guidance.
tasks.approach: Call buildStatefulNotice from experimental.chat.system.transform, prepend the returned notice before the fixed Nexus system prompt, and add explicit allComplete/Step 7 handoff messaging so interactive plan flows naturally into nx_task_add-based task generation.
tasks.acceptance: dynamic plan/run/task notices appear in system prompt injection when relevant; allComplete plan state strongly instructs immediate Step 7 task generation; notice tests cover connected behavior.
tasks.risk: Additional system text may create redundancy with the fixed Nexus prompt; messages must stay short and state-specific.

tasks.title: Differentiate exit enforcement for active vs completed-open cycles
tasks.context: Current exit handling is warning-only. Implement the decided differentiated enforcement: stronger blocking for active cycles and softer but still strong handling for completed-open cycles.
tasks.approach: Strengthen command.execute.before exit guard so active cycles are hard-blocked and completed-open cycles use a strong warning or one-shot soft block. Keep the implementation OpenCode-native rather than trying to reproduce Claude Stop semantics exactly.
tasks.acceptance: exit-like commands are blocked during active cycles; completed-open cycles surface the softer closure-focused enforcement path; regression coverage reflects the split behavior.
tasks.risk: OpenCode command hook capabilities may limit how strong a block can be expressed; UX should avoid trapping the user in an unrecoverable loop.

tasks.title: Refresh Nexus context docs after runtime behavior changes land
tasks.context: These runtime fixes change effective orchestration behavior, prompt injection, lifecycle handling, and cycle enforcement. Core context docs should reflect the final implemented behavior.
tasks.approach: Update the affected .nexus/context documents after code and tests settle, focusing on hooks, architecture, orchestration, and tools behavior that changed in this cycle.
tasks.acceptance: .nexus/context docs reflect the implemented runtime semantics for tracker reset, tool-log attribution, edit gating, dynamic notices, unfinished-task warning, and exit enforcement.
tasks.risk: Documenting before code settles can cause churn, so this task should remain sequenced after implementation and verification.

tasks.title: Add task-after equivalent warning for unfinished owner tasks
tasks.context: OpenCode lacks a SubagentStop hook, but we still want equivalent warning behavior when a subagent finishes and owned tasks remain pending or in_progress.
tasks.approach: At tool.execute.after(task), inspect tasks.json for owner-matched pending/in_progress tasks and inject an equivalent warning or escalation path. Reuse current owner semantics and keep the behavior OpenCode-native rather than Claude-parity literal.
tasks.acceptance: when a subagent task returns while owner tasks remain incomplete, the lead receives a clear warning/escalation notice; no warning is emitted when owner work is fully complete.
tasks.risk: Owner matching by agent type may be too coarse if multiple tasks share the same owner role; later refinement may need owner_agent_id or task linkage.

tasks.title: Verify runtime regressions across tracker, gating, notice, and exit flows
tasks.context: The runtime behavior changes span hooks, state files, and system injection. Verification should cover the new lifecycle and guard semantics end to end.
tasks.approach: Run and extend the focused e2e scripts for tracker reset, tool-log attribution, edit gate scope, stateful notice injection, unfinished-task warning, and exit enforcement. Confirm the new behavior matches the chosen decisions and report any residual ambiguity tied to live OpenCode payloads.
tasks.acceptance: all targeted regression scripts pass and explicitly demonstrate the decided behaviors; any remaining live-runtime uncertainty is documented with precise scope.
tasks.risk: Synthetic hook tests may still leave a small gap relative to real OpenCode session payloads, especially for primary/child session discrimination.

tasks.title: Refresh Nexus context docs after runtime behavior changes land (corrected sequencing)
tasks.context: These runtime fixes change effective orchestration behavior, prompt injection, lifecycle handling, and cycle enforcement. Core context docs should reflect the final implemented behavior once code and verification settle.
tasks.approach: Update the affected .nexus/context documents after implementation and tester verification, focusing on hooks, architecture, orchestration, and tools behavior that changed in this cycle.
tasks.acceptance: .nexus/context docs reflect the implemented runtime semantics for tracker reset, tool-log attribution, edit gating, dynamic notices, unfinished-task warning, and exit enforcement.
tasks.risk: Documenting before code settles can cause churn, so this task is explicitly sequenced after implementation and verification.

cycleTopics: claude-nexus 대비 opencode-nexus의 prompts/hooks/agents/skills 차이 비교 및 선택 논의

tasks.title: Fix tool-log edit detection for apply_patch
tasks.context: User asked under [run] to determine, based on live experiments, how to reliably capture required tool-log information and then fix the tool-log bug. Existing focused test scripts/e2e-tool-log-attribution.mjs defines intended behavior: child-session file edits must append to .nexus/state/opencode-nexus/tool-log.jsonl and later aggregate into agent-tracker files_touched. Live experiment showed spawned engineer agents using apply_patch created files, but tool-log.jsonl remained empty. Relevant files: src/plugin/hooks.ts, src/agents/generated/index.ts, scripts/e2e-tool-log-attribution.mjs, scripts/e2e-init-sync.mjs. Constraint: keep change minimal and preserve generated capability mapping as source-of-truth where possible.
tasks.approach: Implement the smallest runtime fix so edit-like detection also recognizes OpenCode's actual file-edit tool name apply_patch in addition to generated capability-mapped names. Update focused tests to cover apply_patch-based logging, ideally by exercising both existing write behavior and real apply_patch alias behavior without broad refactors.
tasks.acceptance: tool.execute.after treats apply_patch as an edit-like tool; focused e2e proves tool-log.jsonl gets an entry for apply_patch file edits and files_touched still aggregates by child session; build/check relevant tests pass.
tasks.risk: Changing edit classification too broadly could affect edit gate enforcement or internal nexus path exemptions. Keep alias handling narrow and covered by focused tests.

tasks.title: Verify tool-log attribution fix
tasks.context: After the engineer task updates runtime edit detection, verify that tool-log captures child-session edits for apply_patch and that files_touched attribution still works. Relevant checks should focus on the existing attribution e2e and any directly related build/check commands.
tasks.approach: Run targeted verification on the focused e2e plus relevant project checks needed to catch type/runtime regressions in the touched area. Report PASS/FAIL with findings, not code changes.
tasks.acceptance: Verification explicitly states whether apply_patch now appends tool-log entries and whether files_touched attribution still passes for the focused scenario.
tasks.risk: If task IDs are not sequential in this environment, dependency tracking may be approximate; verification should still clearly state what was run and what passed/failed.

tasks.title: Add locked JSON state update helper and adopt it in state mutations
tasks.context: User asked under [run] to fix state-file write races that can drop information in tasks, plan, agent-tracker, and similar state files. Current code uses plain readJsonFile + mutate in memory + writeJsonFile across multiple modules. Evidence: src/shared/json-store.ts only provides raw read/write; src/tools/task.ts nxTaskAdd/nxTaskUpdate do unlocked read-modify-write; src/tools/plan.ts nxPlanStart/nxPlanUpdate/nxPlanDecide do unlocked read-modify-write; src/shared/history.ts appendHistory does unlocked read-modify-write; src/shared/agent-tracker.ts registerInvocationStart/registerInvocationEnd do unlocked read-modify-write. Need a common fix that prevents lost updates when concurrent operations target the same state file.
tasks.approach: Implement a shared file-lock-backed JSON update helper in src/shared/json-store.ts that serializes read-modify-write for a target file, likely via per-file lockfiles with retry plus process-local queue if useful. Migrate agent-tracker, history append, task add/update, and plan start/update/decide to use the helper so IDs and appended records are computed under lock. Keep change minimal and focused on state mutation paths. Add focused tests that reproduce concurrent updates and prove no lost entries/duplicate IDs.
tasks.acceptance: Concurrent updates to the same state JSON file no longer lose entries in agent-tracker/history/tasks/plan mutation paths. Focused tests cover at least agent-tracker and tasks/plan or history append race scenarios. Build and relevant tests pass.
tasks.risk: Cross-file workflows like task close still span multiple files and may not become fully transactional; keep scope on per-file lost-update prevention unless a small safe improvement is obvious.

tasks.title: Verify state-file race fix
tasks.context: After the engineer adds locked JSON mutation handling, verify that concurrent state-file updates no longer lose data. Focus on the state files explicitly mentioned by the user: tasks, plan, agent-tracker, and related history append behavior if changed.
tasks.approach: Run focused verification on new/updated race tests plus any necessary build/type checks. Report PASS/FAIL and residual risks, especially any remaining cross-file non-transactional behavior.
tasks.acceptance: Verification explicitly demonstrates that the previously vulnerable same-file concurrent update paths no longer lose information.
tasks.risk: Full multi-file transaction guarantees may remain out of scope; verification should distinguish same-file race prevention from broader workflow atomicity.

tasks.title: Extend same-file locking to tool-log append/reset
tasks.context: Follow-up to the state-file race fix. The current implementation added an in-process per-file lock queue for JSON read-modify-write updates, but src/shared/tool-log.ts still uses raw fs.appendFile/fs.writeFile without the shared per-file queue. User explicitly asked to include tool-log too if it is similar. Tool-log is append-only, but same-file races can still happen between appendToolLogEntry() and resetToolLog(), and ordering is better made deterministic under the same file queue approach.
tasks.approach: Extend the shared file-mutation queue so tool-log append/reset also serialize by file path. Do this with the smallest shared helper addition possible (for example export generic locked file action or add locked append/write text helpers) and migrate src/shared/tool-log.ts to use it. Add/extend focused concurrency coverage to prove concurrent appends survive and reset/append ordering is serialized as intended.
tasks.acceptance: tool-log append/reset use the same per-file serialization mechanism as other state-file mutations; focused tests cover concurrent tool-log appends and/or reset-vs-append ordering sufficiently to show no dropped lines under same-process concurrency; build and focused tests pass.
tasks.risk: Keep scope to same-process same-file serialization; do not broaden into speculative cross-process locking unless necessary.

tasks.title: Verify tool-log locking extension
tasks.context: After tool-log append/reset are moved onto the shared same-file serialization layer, verify that focused concurrency coverage passes and the runtime still preserves tool-log lines under concurrent appends.
tasks.approach: Run focused verification for the new tool-log race coverage plus existing state concurrency coverage as needed. Report PASS/FAIL and any residual risk, especially same-process vs cross-process scope.
tasks.acceptance: Verification explicitly states whether concurrent tool-log append behavior is now serialized and whether focused tests pass.
tasks.risk: The implementation may intentionally remain same-process only; verification should call that out rather than treating it as a bug if unchanged.

plan.topic: Extend nx-setup to configure built-in agent models via provider->model flow

issues.title: Which built-in agents should nx-setup expose for explicit model assignment, and how should disabled or hidden built-ins be treated?
issues.decision: Expose only user-meaningful built-in subagents in the default nx-setup model flow. Add `general` and `explore` to the built-in model selection surface, keep hidden/system built-ins (`compaction`, `title`, `summary`) out of the default flow, and exclude disabled built-ins such as `build` and `plan` unless they are enabled in the effective config. This keeps setup focused on practical cost control without turning it into an internal runtime panel.

issues.title: How should the setup wizard and backend represent provider->model selection so built-in agents can use cheaper models without conflicting with current Nexus tiering?
issues.decision: Keep `models` as the canonical backend shape and stop treating `lead_model` + `toStandardModel()` as the primary tiered flow. In the setup wizard, tiered selection should explicitly choose a high-capability model for `nexus` + HOW and a standard model for DO + CHECK. Built-in `general` and `explore` should inherit the standard tier by default, with an optional extra provider->model override step that persists to `models.agents.general` and `models.agents.explore`. Legacy `lead_model` and heuristic standard-model derivation remain only as backward-compatibility fallbacks.

plan.research_summary: Reviewed the current setup wizard template, setup backend, runtime config hook, e2e coverage, and official OpenCode docs. Current nx_setup model assignment only targets nexus plus Nexus catalog agents from AGENT_META; built-in agents build/plan/general/explore/compaction/title/summary are untouched. The current skill text already promises provider-first selection and says tiered should ask twice, but the backend only accepts a single lead_model and derives the standard model heuristically with toStandardModel(). Official OpenCode docs confirm built-in agents can be customized through config and per-agent model overrides via agent.<name>.model. Repo-local opencode.json manually disables build and plan, creating a possible UX mismatch if setup starts assigning models to disabled built-ins. Main implementation touchpoints are src/tools/setup.ts, templates/skills/nx-setup/SKILL.md, generated nx-setup skill output, and scripts/e2e-setup-template.mjs. create-config.ts injects Nexus agents only and does not manage built-in agent model defaults.

tasks.title: Implement built-in agent model assignment in nx_setup
tasks.context: Plan decisions: default built-in setup surface includes only general and explore; build/plan only if enabled; compaction/title/summary excluded from default flow. Keep models as canonical backend shape. Tiered flow should explicitly represent high-capability nexus+HOW and standard DO+CHECK. general/explore inherit the standard tier by default, with optional override persisted via models.agents.general and models.agents.explore. Legacy lead_model + toStandardModel remains fallback-only.
tasks.approach: Update src/tools/setup.ts so built-in agent model assignment is first-class in merge logic. Add effective handling for general/explore inheritance from DO/CHECK, allow explicit overrides via models.agents, and ensure disabled built-ins are not written in the default path. Preserve backward compatibility for older lead_model-driven calls while making explicit models the canonical path.
tasks.acceptance: nx_setup can persist explicit model choices for general/explore, tiered setup no longer depends on heuristic standard-model derivation for the main path, and disabled/hidden built-ins are not unintentionally surfaced or written by default.
tasks.risk: Drift between legacy and canonical inputs could produce surprising model assignments; writing config for disabled built-ins could create misleading state.

tasks.title: Update nx-setup wizard instructions for built-in model flow
tasks.context: The current nx-setup skill text already describes provider-first selection but still documents tiered as a single lead_model input. It needs to reflect the new built-in model policy and explicit provider->model flow for high-capability, standard, and optional built-in override selection.
tasks.approach: Revise templates/skills/nx-setup/SKILL.md and any generated counterpart so the user-facing setup flow explains: general/explore are the only built-ins in the default flow, they inherit the standard tier by default, and there is an optional extra provider->model override step for those built-ins. Remove or demote language that implies heuristic derivation is the primary path.
tasks.acceptance: Wizard instructions are consistent with backend behavior and plan decisions, and clearly explain when built-in model override is optional versus inherited.
tasks.risk: Template/generated file drift may leave stale instructions in one location.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for built-in setup model behavior
tasks.context: Existing e2e setup tests cover Nexus agent model groups and legacy overrides, but not built-in general/explore inheritance or exclusion behavior.
tasks.approach: Extend scripts/e2e-setup-template.mjs to verify: general/explore can receive explicit model assignments through canonical setup inputs, they inherit the standard tier by default in tiered/custom flows, and default setup behavior does not write hidden/system or disabled built-ins unintentionally.
tasks.acceptance: Regression tests fail without the new behavior and pass with it, covering both canonical explicit inputs and backward-compatible fallback behavior.
tasks.risk: Tests may overfit current repo-local disable state or generated config defaults.

plan.topic: Prevent recursive subagent spawning from subagents

issues.title: Restrict subagent spawning so only the primary nexus agent can invoke the task tool
issues.decision: Use defense in depth. First, remove task-tool access from all configured subagents so the model is not encouraged or able to delegate further. Second, enforce the same rule at runtime in the task hook by resolving the caller from explicit agent fields or from the current session's agent-tracker entry and rejecting any task invocation whose caller is not the primary nexus agent. This is preferred over prompt-only guidance because prompt constraints are insufficient, and over hook-only enforcement because hiding the tool reduces accidental recursion and makes the capability boundary explicit.

plan.research_summary: Inspected the current task/delegation enforcement. The task tool is intercepted in src/plugin/hooks.ts by enforceTaskTeamPolicy(), but that function is effectively a no-op for known Nexus agents. Subagent lead-only enforcement already exists for nx_task_close via both direct caller inspection and session-to-agent-tracker lookup in hooks.ts and src/tools/task.ts. Subagent tool visibility is configured in src/create-config.ts; today subagents explicitly hide nx_task_close but not task. Existing e2e coverage includes scripts/e2e-team-policy.mjs for task-hook policy and scripts/e2e-subagent-prompts.mjs for subagent tool exposure. This suggests the safest fix is defense in depth: hide task from all subagents in config and hard-block task invocations from any non-primary session in the hook, while allowing the primary nexus agent to continue delegating.

tasks.title: Implement primary-only task delegation policy
tasks.context: User reported recursive subagent spawning because subagents can call the task tool. Current relevant files: src/plugin/hooks.ts has enforceTaskTeamPolicy() but it is effectively a no-op for known Nexus agents; src/create-config.ts hides nx_task_close from subagents but does not hide task; scripts/e2e-team-policy.mjs covers hook-side task policy; scripts/e2e-subagent-prompts.mjs covers subagent tool visibility. Primary agent id is nexus in src/agents/primary.ts.
tasks.approach: Apply defense in depth: update subagent tool policy so task is disabled for all subagents, and implement a runtime hook check that rejects task invocations from any caller/session that resolves to a non-primary agent. Reuse the existing nx_task_close lead-only session lookup pattern where practical, keep the change minimal, and add/update e2e coverage for both the hook gate and config exposure.
tasks.acceptance: 1. Subagent configs expose task:false for every non-primary Nexus agent. 2. A task invocation issued from a non-primary agent session is rejected with a clear primary-only error. 3. A task invocation from the primary agent remains allowed. 4. Existing and new e2e coverage for team policy/subagent prompts passes.
tasks.risk: Overly broad gating could block legitimate primary delegation if caller detection is wrong; keep fallback resolution narrow and test both primary and subagent cases.

tasks.title: Verify primary-only task delegation policy
tasks.context: Verification for the new primary-only task delegation policy once implementation is complete. Relevant acceptance is stored on task 1.
tasks.approach: Run the targeted e2e scripts that cover subagent tool exposure and task-hook team policy. Confirm that subagents cannot invoke task while the primary nexus agent still can.
tasks.acceptance: Verification reports PASS for the updated task policy checks with no unresolved failures.
tasks.risk: Targeted e2e tests may miss unrelated regressions; this task only verifies the delegation policy change.

The relevant local files are `src/agents/primary.ts`, `src/plugin/system-prompt.ts`, and `src/plugin/hooks.ts` (`buildStatefulNotice`, mode/state handling around `experimental.chat.system.transform`). The correct refactor is to reduce duplicated phrasing, centralize shared prompt context calculation, and keep existing runtime steering semantics and test contracts intact. There is an unrelated local modification in `.nexus/history.json`; do not revert or include unrelated changes.

plan.topic: OpenCode 글로벌 설정과 nx-setup user scope 검증

issues.title: nx-setup의 user/global scope 구현이 OpenCode 공식 경로 모델과 일치하는가?
issues.decision: `nx-setup`의 user/global scope 구현은 OpenCode 공식 경로 모델과 일치한다. `src/tools/setup.ts`는 user scope를 `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`, `~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md`, `~/.config/opencode/skills/`로 해석하며, 이는 OpenCode 공식 Config/Skills 문서의 global config 및 global skills 경로와 부합한다. 다만 이 scope는 npm 설치 범위가 아니라 runtime config/instructions/skills 자산의 배치 범위로 설명해야 혼동이 없다.

issues.title: OpenCode의 글로벌 설정에서 npm은 필수인가, 아니면 선택적 설치 수단인가?
issues.decision: OpenCode의 글로벌 설정에서 npm은 필수가 아니라 선택적 설치/배포 수단이다. 공식 Intro 문서는 OpenCode 자체를 install script, npm, bun, pnpm, yarn, brew 등 여러 방식으로 설치할 수 있다고 설명한다. 공식 Config 문서는 글로벌 설정의 정답 경로를 `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`로 정의하며, Plugins 문서는 npm plugin을 `plugin` 배열로 선언하면 OpenCode가 Bun으로 설치한다고 설명한다. 따라서 'global 설정 = npm'은 부정확하고, 더 정확한 표현은 'global 설정은 `~/.config/opencode` 아래 파일/디렉터리이며 npm은 그 위에서 쓰일 수 있는 설치/배포 채널 중 하나'이다.

issues.title: npm 기반 plugin 버전이 올라가면 OpenCode가 자동으로 최신 버전으로 갱신하는가?
issues.decision: 현재 OpenCode 구현 기준으로 npm 기반 plugin이 새 버전으로 올라가도 자동으로 npm 최신 버전으로 갱신된다고 보면 안 된다. `packages/opencode/src/plugin/shared.ts`는 bare plugin spec를 내부적으로 `package@latest`로 해석하지만, `packages/opencode/src/npm/index.ts`의 `Npm.add()`는 해당 spec용 캐시 디렉터리에서 `arborist.loadVirtual()`이 성공하면 기존 설치를 그대로 반환하고 registry 최신 버전 확인이나 재설치를 하지 않는다. 따라서 최초 설치나 캐시 미스 때는 최신이 설치되지만, 이후에는 같은 spec에 대해 캐시를 재사용하므로 명시적 업데이트나 캐시 제거 없이는 새 npm 배포를 자동 추적하지 않는 현재 동작으로 해석하는 것이 맞다.

issues.title: CLI-first 설치/업데이트 흐름으로 전환하고 nx-setup 제거
issues.decision: 설치와 업데이트는 플러그인 로드 전에도 실행 가능한 CLI가 맡고, README는 CLI-first 흐름으로 개편한다. `nx-setup`과 관련 스킬/도구 노출은 제거하고, 설치 범위(scope), plugin spec 기록, 업데이트/캐시 갱신 같은 결정적 작업은 CLI에서 처리한다. 프로젝트 분석처럼 LLM이 유리한 작업은 `nx-init`에 남긴다.

plan.research_summary: 리포지토리의 nx_setup 구현과 nx-setup 스킬, e2e 테스트를 검토했고, 공식 OpenCode 문서의 Config/Plugins/Intro 페이지를 대조했다. 구현상 nx_setup의 user scope는 `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`, `~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md`, `~/.config/opencode/skills/`를 대상으로 하며, plugin 등록은 `opencode.json`의 `plugin` 배열에 `opencode-nexus`를 추가하는 방식이다. 공식 문서도 글로벌 config 경로를 `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`, 글로벌 plugin 디렉터리를 `~/.config/opencode/plugins/`로 명시한다. npm은 OpenCode 자체 설치 수단 중 하나이며, npm plugins는 config의 `plugin` 배열을 통해 선언되고 Bun이 startup 시 설치/캐시한다. 다만 published plugin이 startup 때마다 자동으로 최신 버전으로 갱신되는지는 공식 문서에서 명시하지 않는다.

tasks.title: Clarify nx-setup scope wording
tasks.context: The plan concluded that `nx-setup` user/global scope is correct, but the wording should distinguish runtime asset placement (`opencode.json`, `AGENTS.md`, `skills/`) from plugin installation/loading. Relevant evidence: `src/tools/setup.ts`, `src/skills/generated/nx-setup.ts`, OpenCode docs for Config and Skills.
tasks.approach: Update setup-facing documentation or skill copy so `user`/`project` scope is described as runtime config/instructions/skills scope, not npm installation scope.
tasks.acceptance: Documentation clearly states that `user` scope writes Nexus runtime assets under `~/.config/opencode/`, and does not imply that npm global install defines config scope.
tasks.risk: If wording remains ambiguous, users may continue to conflate plugin loading with config placement.

tasks.title: Review nx-setup scope clarification
tasks.context: Verification task for any documentation or prompt wording changes around `nx-setup` user/global scope semantics.
tasks.approach: Check that the updated wording matches the actual implementation and official OpenCode config/skills/plugin docs, and that it no longer implies npm global install defines config scope.
tasks.acceptance: Review confirms the wording is accurate, non-misleading, and grounded in current code/doc behavior.
tasks.risk: A partial wording fix could still leave room for users to misread the setup flow.

tasks.title: Document plugin update expectation
tasks.context: The plan concluded that OpenCode currently caches npm plugin installs per spec and does not automatically refresh to newly published npm latest versions for already-cached plugins. Evidence from OpenCode source: `packages/opencode/src/plugin/shared.ts`, `packages/opencode/src/npm/index.ts`.
tasks.approach: Add documentation or troubleshooting guidance that plugin updates should not be assumed to auto-follow npm latest after first install; describe that explicit refresh or cache invalidation may be needed.
tasks.acceptance: Documentation states the current update behavior accurately and distinguishes OpenCode self-update from npm plugin update behavior.
tasks.risk: Without this clarification, users may wrongly assume new npm releases are picked up automatically.

tasks.title: Review plugin update documentation
tasks.context: Verification task for documentation about cached npm plugin installs and lack of automatic latest refresh behavior.
tasks.approach: Review the wording against current OpenCode docs and source behavior, ensuring no overclaim beyond the code evidence.
tasks.acceptance: Review confirms the update guidance is accurate, scoped to current implementation, and does not conflate plugin updates with OpenCode autoupdate.
tasks.risk: Overstating the behavior as a guaranteed long-term contract could become stale if OpenCode changes implementation.

tasks.title: Implement CLI install/update and remove nx-setup
tasks.context: User requested `[run] cli 기능 추가 + README에 설치 흐름 개편 + nx-setup 제거`. Current package has no CLI (`package.json` exports only plugin entry), `src/tools/setup.ts` implements `nx_setup`, README still tells users to add `plugin` manually then run `nx-setup`, and templates/generated skills expose `nx-setup`. Prior decisions: installation/update should move to a CLI-first deterministic flow; `nx-setup` should be removed; CLI should handle scope, plugin spec recording, and update behavior; `nx-init` remains for LLM-friendly project analysis. Relevant files likely include `package.json`, `src/tools/index.ts`, `src/index.ts`, `src/create-config.ts`, `src/tools/setup.ts`, skill templates/generated skill metadata, tests under `scripts/`, and README-related references.
tasks.approach: Add a packaged CLI entrypoint that can install/update `opencode-nexus` into user/project OpenCode config, preferably with explicit version pinning for deterministic updates. Remove `nx_setup` tool exposure and the `nx-setup` skill/template plumbing. Update or add tests for the new CLI behavior and for removal of setup skill/tool exposure. Keep changes minimal but complete.
tasks.acceptance: Package exposes a working CLI entrypoint for install/update flows. Runtime no longer exposes `nx_setup`/`nx-setup`. Build and relevant tests pass after the migration. Existing `nx-init`/`nx-sync`/plan-run flows keep working.
tasks.risk: Removing setup touches plugin init, generated skills, docs, and tests; partial removal could leave broken references or stale generated artifacts.

README currently centers `nx-setup`; prior plan tasks 1 and 3 also called for clarifying runtime scope and plugin update expectations. Documentation should absorb those clarifications into the new CLI-first flow. Relevant files: `README.md`, `README.en.md`, and any other user-facing installation references touched by the code migration.
tasks.approach: Document install/update around the new CLI commands, explain user/project scope there, and clarify that OpenCode startup does not automatically refresh cached npm plugin versions. Remove `nx-setup` references from README-level onboarding while keeping `nx-init` as the LLM-oriented next step.

plan.topic: CLI interactive install/update와 global version flag 보강

issues.title: CLI는 전역 `--version`/`-v`와 어떤 interactive 기본 동작을 제공해야 하는가?
issues.decision: CLI는 top-level `--version`/`-v`를 지원해 패키지 버전을 즉시 출력해야 한다. `install`과 `update`는 TTY에서 실행되고 관련 선택 플래그가 빠진 경우 create-react-app 스타일의 interactive prompt로 scope, pin 여부, 버전 입력 같은 선택을 묻고, 명시 플래그가 주어진 비대화식 호출은 기존처럼 그대로 동작해야 한다. 비-TTY에서는 자동 질문을 시도하지 말고 현재의 결정적 동작을 유지한다.

plan.research_summary: 현재 `src/cli.ts`는 `install`/`update` 두 서브커맨드만 파싱하며, 전역 `--version`/`-v` 처리가 없어 `bun dist/cli.js --version`이 unknown command로 실패한다. `install`은 인자 없이 실행되면 기본값(`scope=user`, pin=true)으로 즉시 `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`을 수정한다. 테스트는 `scripts/e2e-setup-template.mjs`에서 비대화식 install/update와 `--no-pin`만 검증한다. 사용자 요구는 create-react-app 같은 대화형 선택지 기반 install/update UX와 version 표면 보강이다.

tasks.title: Implement interactive CLI prompts and version flag
tasks.context: User reported two CLI UX issues after the recent CLI-first migration: `bun dist/cli.js --version` fails with `Unknown command: --version`, and `bun dist/cli.js install` immediately writes to user config instead of offering a create-react-app-like interactive selection flow. Current implementation is in `src/cli.ts`; current CLI tests are in `scripts/e2e-setup-template.mjs`. The repo is already dirty from the prior CLI migration; preserve existing user/agent changes. Active plan decision: support top-level `--version`/`-v`, and make `install`/`update` interactive in TTY when relevant flags are omitted while preserving deterministic non-interactive behavior when flags are provided or stdin is not a TTY.
tasks.approach: Add top-level version handling plus a minimal interactive prompt flow using Node readline for `install`/`update`. Prompt for scope and pin/version choices when running interactively without explicit flags; keep existing flag-based behavior unchanged. Extend CLI tests to cover the new behavior, including `--version` and interactive flows via scripted stdin.
tasks.acceptance: `bun dist/cli.js --version` prints the package version and exits successfully. `install` and `update` prompt interactively in a TTY when options are omitted, and still behave deterministically with explicit flags. Updated CLI tests pass.
tasks.risk: Interactive CLI logic can easily break non-interactive automation if TTY detection or prompt defaults are wrong.

tasks.title: Update CLI docs for interactive flow
tasks.context: After CLI behavior changes land, docs should reflect the new top-level `--version` support and the interactive default flow for `install`/`update`. Current user-facing docs live mainly in `README.md` and `README.en.md` and currently show only non-interactive examples.
tasks.approach: Update the README install/update section to mention that bare `install`/`update` can prompt interactively in a terminal, while flags keep the flow non-interactive. Add a brief note or example for `--version` if appropriate.
tasks.acceptance: README docs match the shipped CLI behavior in both Korean and English and no longer suggest that `install` always writes immediately without prompts in a terminal.
tasks.risk: Docs can drift from the actual prompt flow if updated before the implementation stabilizes.

tasks.title: Verify interactive CLI behavior
tasks.context: Need to verify the CLI fix for top-level version handling and interactive install/update prompts after implementation and doc updates land. Existing CLI coverage is in `scripts/e2e-setup-template.mjs`.
tasks.approach: Run build/check and the relevant CLI-focused tests, then spot-check the new interactive behavior and doc alignment. Confirm that non-interactive explicit-flag calls still work.
tasks.acceptance: Verification confirms `--version` works, interactive terminal flows behave as documented, and existing deterministic CLI invocations still pass.
tasks.risk: TTY-sensitive behavior may pass in one harness path but fail in another if the test setup is not representative.

tasks.title: Implement CLI version flag and interactive prompts with tests
tasks.context: Apply decided behavior: top-level --version/-v support, TTY-only interactive install/update when relevant flags omitted, preserve non-interactive deterministic explicit-flag flows.
tasks.approach: Inspect current src/cli.ts and e2e script, add minimal prompt helper with Node readline, wire parsing for top-level version alias, update e2e tests for version and scripted interactive cases, run build/check/tests.
tasks.acceptance: CLI supports --version/-v at top level; install/update prompt in TTY when choices omitted; explicit flags/non-TTY keep prior deterministic behavior; CLI-focused tests pass.
tasks.risk: Dirty worktree and existing migration changes could overlap; interactive tests can be flaky if stdin/tty handling differs.

tasks.title: Fix workflow Node 24 warning
tasks.context: The publish workflow emitted a GitHub Actions warning that `actions/checkout@v4` and `actions/setup-node@v4` are still running on Node.js 20. The annotation explicitly recommends setting `FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true` in the workflow to opt in now. User asked to fix the workflow warning, commit, and push.
tasks.approach: Make the smallest safe workflow-only change by setting `FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true` at workflow scope so the existing actions run on Node 24 without changing action major versions during a release follow-up.
tasks.acceptance: `.github/workflows/publish-npm.yml` opts JavaScript actions into Node 24 and the change is committed and pushed.
tasks.risk: Changing action major versions right after a successful release would be higher risk than the explicit env opt-in recommended by GitHub.

plan.topic: opencode-nexus CLI 도움말/인터랙션 UX 개선

issues.title: 도움말을 top-level / command-level로 어떻게 분리할지
issues.decision: 도움말 UX는 B안으로 간다. top-level help와 command-level help를 분리한다. top-level help는 사용 가능한 커맨드 목록과 간단한 설명, 진입 예시만 보여주고, `install --help`와 `update --help`는 해당 커맨드의 사용법, 관련 옵션, 예시만 보여준다. 내부적으로는 `help + optional command` 형태로 파싱/렌더링 맥락을 보존해 향후 command metadata 레지스트리로 확장 가능하도록 한다. 현재 규모에서는 별도 무거운 registry 도입은 보류한다.

issues.title: 인터랙티브 선택 UI를 어떤 방식으로 구현할지
issues.decision: interactive selection UI는 C안으로 간다. standalone CLI의 선택형 질문은 기본적으로 내장 raw TTY 기반 single-select UI를 사용해 방향키(Up/Down)로 이동하고 Enter로 확정한다. 다만 raw mode를 사용할 수 없거나 비TTY/제한된 실행 환경에서는 현재의 숫자/문자 입력 기반 promptChoice 흐름으로 fallback한다. 이 결정은 외부 프롬프트 라이브러리 도입 없이도 install/update/setup의 현재 범위를 커버하면서, 실제 터미널 사용자에게는 기대에 맞는 방향키 선택 UX를 제공하고 테스트/자동화 환경 호환성도 유지하기 위한 것이다.

issues.title: task delegation guard를 fail-closed로 바꾸고 caller provenance가 없으면 기본 차단할지
issues.decision: `task` delegation guard는 독립 안건으로 유지하되, 원인 분석상 issue #5와 연동된 문제로 다룬다. 대응은 둘 다 수행한다. (1) `createConfigHook()`를 additive merge로 바꿔 사용자가 `agent.<id>`에 `mode/model`만 명시해도 Nexus 기본 `description/prompt/tools`가 누락되지 않게 하고, 특히 모든 서브에이전트에 `tools.task = false`와 `tools.nx_task_close = false`가 merge 이후에도 항상 적용되게 한다. (2) 런타임 `task` delegation guard는 fail-open이 아니라 fail-closed로 강화한다. caller provenance(`agent`, `sessionID` 등)가 확인되지 않으면 기본 차단하고, Nexus lead로 확정 가능한 경우에만 `task`를 허용한다. 즉 1차 방어는 config-level tool exposure 차단, 2차 방어는 runtime-level fail-closed enforcement로 이중화한다. 또한 회귀 방지를 위해 기존 agent partial override가 있는 config에서도 subagent `task`/`nx_task_close` 차단이 유지되는 테스트와, caller metadata가 없을 때 `task`가 거부되는 테스트를 추가한다.

issues.title: standalone CLI가 모델 설정까지 담당할지, 아니면 설치와 설정을 분리할지
issues.decision: standalone CLI의 책임은 두 단계로 분리한다. `install`/`update`는 플러그인 등록과 버전 관리에 집중하고, 모델 설정은 별도의 `setup` 명령이 담당한다. 다만 사용성 관점에서 `install` 성공 직후 사용자가 원하면 즉시 `setup` 흐름으로 이어질 수 있게 handoff를 제공한다. `setup`은 user/project scope의 `opencode.json`에 대한 모델 선택 보조 도구로 한정하며, 주 책임은 `agent.*.model` 값을 쓰는 것이다. prompt/tools/description 같은 Nexus 기본 주입까지 CLI가 직접 소유하지 않는다. 이 분리는 현재 help surface를 작게 유지하면서도 모델 설정을 first-class로 노출할 수 있게 한다.

issues.title: createConfigHook가 기존 agent 엔트리에 additive merge를 수행해 partial user overrides를 안전하게 지원할지
issues.decision: `createConfigHook()`는 기존 `agent.<id>` 엔트리를 replace/skip하지 않고 additive merge를 수행한다. 사용자가 명시한 `mode`, `model`, `description`, `prompt`, `tools` 등 기존 값은 우선 보존하고, 빠진 필드만 Nexus 기본값으로 채운다. top-level agent 엔트리는 field-by-field merge를 적용하며, `tools`는 객체 전체 교체가 아니라 key-by-key merge를 적용한다. 서브에이전트에 대한 `task=false`와 `nx_task_close=false` 가드는 merge 이후에도 항상 강제되어 사용자 설정으로 우회되지 않게 한다. 이 결정의 목적은 사용자가 `opencode.json`에 모델이나 일부 필드만 명시해도 Nexus 기본 prompt/tools/description이 안전하게 유지되도록 하는 것이다.

plan.research_summary: 현재 CLI 구현은 src/cli.ts 단일 파일에 집중되어 있다. 도움말은 printHelp() 하나로 top-level과 command-level을 구분하지 않고 'install'과 'update'의 옵션/예시를 한 번에 출력한다(src/cli.ts:44-69, parseArgs 72-160). 또한 command 뒤의 --help도 별도 커맨드 맥락 없이 동일한 전역 help로 귀결된다(src/cli.ts:107-109). 인터랙티브 입력은 node:readline/promises 기반이며, promptChoice()가 번호 목록을 출력하고 사용자가 숫자나 value 문자열을 직접 입력해야 한다(src/cli.ts:199-230). 화살표 이동/엔터 선택을 지원하는 TUI형 선택 UI는 현재 없다. 패키지 의존성에는 관련 프롬프트 라이브러리가 없고(package.json:49-59), 기존 e2e는 숫자 입력 패턴을 전제로 CLI를 검증한다(scripts/e2e-setup-template.mjs:13-62, 118-126).

tasks.title: Implement CLI help, setup command, and interactive selection UX
tasks.context: Apply plan decisions from issues #1, #2, and #4. Relevant files: src/cli.ts, scripts/e2e-setup-template.mjs, and any minimal CLI-facing docs if needed. Current CLI only supports install/update, prints one combined help block, and uses numeric readline choice input. Decided direction: top-level vs command-level help split; add separate setup command for model-only configuration with install->setup handoff; implement raw TTY single-select with fallback to current text input when raw mode/TTY is unavailable.
tasks.approach: Keep the change minimal and centered in src/cli.ts. Introduce command-aware help rendering, add setup parsing/execution for scope + model selection that writes agent.<id>.model values into opencode.json, preserve install/update as plugin/version operations, and add a prompt primitive that uses raw-TTY arrow-key selection with Enter but falls back to current readline promptChoice behavior in constrained environments. Update or extend e2e coverage for help/setup/interactive fallback paths.
tasks.acceptance:
- `opencode-nexus --help` shows only top-level commands and relevant global help
- `opencode-nexus install --help`, `update --help`, and `setup --help` show only command-relevant usage/options/examples
- CLI supports `setup` as a separate command for model-only configuration in user/project scope
- `install` can hand off to `setup` interactively without forcing it in non-interactive mode
- Interactive single-select uses arrow keys + Enter when raw TTY is available and falls back safely otherwise
- Existing non-interactive install/update behavior remains compatible, with relevant tests updated or added
tasks.risk: Most risk is concentrated in terminal state handling and maintaining scriptable behavior while adding setup. Keep fallback behavior explicit and avoid broad CLI refactors.

tasks.title: Harden config merge and fail-closed task delegation guard
tasks.context: Apply plan decisions from issues #3 and #5. Relevant files: src/create-config.ts, src/plugin/hooks.ts, scripts/e2e-subagent-prompts.mjs, scripts/e2e-team-policy.mjs. Current createConfigHook skips any pre-existing agent entry entirely, so user-defined mode/model can prevent prompt/tools/description injection. Current runtime task guard blocks some nested task calls but is fail-open when caller provenance like sessionID is absent.
tasks.approach: Refactor createConfigHook to perform additive field-by-field merge for existing agent entries, with key-by-key merge for tools and hard enforcement of subagent `task=false` and `nx_task_close=false`. Keep user-provided mode/model/prompt/description/tool overrides unless a safety invariant must be enforced. In hooks, change task delegation enforcement to fail-closed: if caller provenance cannot prove Nexus lead ownership, reject the task call. Add regression coverage for partial agent overrides and for task calls with missing caller metadata.
tasks.acceptance:
- Existing `agent.<id>` entries receive missing Nexus defaults via additive merge instead of being skipped wholesale
- User-specified mode/model values remain intact after config hook execution
- All subagents end up with `tools.task === false` and `tools.nx_task_close === false`, including when agent entries already exist
- Runtime `task` delegation is rejected when caller provenance is missing or cannot be resolved to Nexus lead
- Regression tests cover both partial agent override merge and fail-closed task enforcement
tasks.risk: Behavior changes in config merging can affect self-hosting or user-customized agent entries; keep merge additive and narrowly scoped. Guard hardening may surface hidden nested task usage in existing tests.

tasks.title: Verify CLI and guard hardening changes
tasks.context: Verification task after the two engineer tasks complete. Targets the CLI command surface changes and the config/guard hardening changes. Relevant checks likely include setup/help e2e scripts and nested-task policy regressions.
tasks.approach: Review the changed files, run the smallest meaningful automated checks that cover the acceptance criteria, and report PASS/FAIL with concrete findings. Reopen implementation only if acceptance criteria are not met.
tasks.acceptance:
- Command-level help/setup behavior matches the accepted plan decisions
- Partial agent overrides still receive required Nexus defaults and subagent task restrictions
- Missing-provenance task calls are denied
- Relevant automated tests/build checks pass or failures are reported concretely
tasks.risk: Verification may need to distinguish raw-TTY-only behavior from fallback behavior in headless test environments.

cycleTopics: opencode-nexus CLI 도움말/인터랙션 UX 개선
Provide an explicit verification pass over both CLI and guard changes.

tasks.title: Relax task guard and extend config-level blocking
tasks.context: User wants [run] change focused on recursive task prevention: relax runtime task delegation guard so missing/unverifiable provenance no longer blocks Nexus lead, and extend config-level tool blocking so built-in general/explore also have tools.task=false and tools.nx_task_close=false. User does not care about custom agents.
tasks.approach: Adjust runtime task guard to block only explicit non-nexus callers or verified non-nexus child sessions; allow unknown/unverifiable provenance. Update config hook to enforce task/task_close denial on general and explore agent entries in addition to Nexus subagents. Add/repair tests covering relaxed guard behavior and built-in agent tool blocking.
tasks.acceptance:
- task guard no longer rejects task calls solely because caller provenance is missing or unverifiable
- explicit non-nexus callers and verified child subagent callers are still rejected for task delegation
- config hook enforces tools.task=false and tools.nx_task_close=false for general and explore agents
- tests cover relaxed guard semantics and config-level blocking for general/explore
- build and relevant e2e tests pass
tasks.risk: Main risk is over-relaxing guard and allowing unintended callers; mitigate by keeping explicit non-nexus and verified child-session blocks while limiting scope to recursive-task prevention.

cycleTopics: Restore lead task delegation while keeping recursion protection and extending config safety for general/explore.

plan.topic: CLI setup command redesign for grouped model configuration and dynamic provider discovery

issues.title: What should the setup command's primary interaction model be: grouped-by-role defaults first, with optional all-at-once flow, or a different default?
issues.decision: Adopt Option A. The CLI setup command should default to grouped-by-role guided configuration, with an optional all-at-once single-model shortcut for users who want speed. The grouped default should map directly to the meaningful setup surface: nexus primary, HOW, DO, CHECK, and built-in general/explore. The all-at-once path should be implemented as a convenience wrapper over the same canonical grouped config semantics, not as a separate persistence model.

issues.title: How should the CLI discover providers/models dynamically from the local OpenCode environment, and what should the fallback behavior be when discovery is partial or unavailable?
issues.decision: Adopt Option A. The CLI setup command should use a discovery-first runtime pipeline based on the local OpenCode environment: use `opencode providers list` as the primary provider inventory source, `opencode debug config` as context for existing/resolved model values and prefills, and `opencode models <provider>` for concrete model candidates per selected provider. Fallback must be explicit and progressive: if provider discovery is empty or unavailable, ask the user to choose a provider manually; if model enumeration fails for one provider, degrade only that provider to manual model entry; if all discovery is unavailable, fall back to full manual provider/model entry. Do not rely on a hardcoded provider catalog as the primary UX path.

issues.title: What config write semantics should grouped setup use for Nexus/HOW/DO/CHECK/general/explore so it aligns with existing agent config merge behavior and prior setup decisions?
issues.decision: Adopt Option A. Grouped CLI setup should persist only targeted concrete `agent.<id>.model` values for the surfaced setup roles it owns: `nexus`, HOW agents, DO agents, CHECK agents, and built-in `general`/`explore`. It should preserve all other agent fields and leave unrelated, disabled, hidden, or custom agents untouched by default, including `build` and `plan`. Grouping and inheritance are setup-time concepts only; persistence should remain concrete and OpenCode-native. `general` and `explore` should conceptually inherit the standard tier by default during setup, but the resolved model values should be written explicitly to `agent.general.model` and `agent.explore.model` rather than introducing persistent group metadata or dynamic inheritance semantics.

issues.title: What command/flag/interactive UX should support both guided grouped setup and scriptable non-interactive usage without breaking current CLI expectations?
issues.decision: Adopt Option A. Keep `setup` as the single CLI command for model configuration. The default interactive UX should be a guided grouped setup flow with discovery-first provider/model selection, while non-interactive usage should support explicit grouped flags for scriptable configuration. Add `--all-model` as the preferred explicit all-at-once shortcut, keep existing `--model` behavior as a backward-compatible alias for the same semantics, and allow more specific grouped flags to override the all-at-once baseline. Preserve command-specific help and keep interactive and scripted usage as two entry styles for the same `setup` command rather than splitting into subcommands.

plan.research_summary: Current CLI `setup` is implemented only in `src/cli.ts` and is intentionally very narrow: it accepts a single `--model` value, prompts for one free-text model string in interactive mode, and then writes that same value into every `agent.<id>.model` entry via `applySetupModel()` (`src/cli.ts:757-790`). There is no provider discovery, no per-role grouping, and no distinction between Nexus/HOW/DO/CHECK/general/explore in the CLI today. Existing e2e coverage (`scripts/e2e-setup-template.mjs:137-161`) asserts this current single-model behavior. Recent plan history is relevant: plan #41 decided that built-in `general` and `explore` should be part of the setup model surface, that `models`/grouped assignment is the canonical conceptual shape, and that `general`/`explore` should inherit the standard tier by default with optional override (`.nexus/history.json:7857-7879`). OpenCode CLI capabilities also support dynamic discovery in this environment: `opencode providers list` returns configured/authenticated providers, and `opencode debug config` shows resolved agent model state including current `general`/`explore` values and existing per-agent overrides. That means the user’s concern about hardcoded providers is directionally correct for the CLI surface: the current CLI does not inspect local provider state at all, even though the platform exposes commands that could support discovery-first UX.

tasks.title: Implement grouped discovery-first CLI setup redesign
tasks.context: Implement plan #46 decisions across issues 1-4. Current `src/cli.ts` setup flow only supports a single `--model` that broadcasts to all agents, and existing e2e coverage in `scripts/e2e-setup-template.mjs` asserts that behavior. Relevant prior decisions: grouped-by-role guided setup is the default interactive UX; discovery should use `opencode providers list`, `opencode debug config`, and `opencode models <provider>` with explicit fallback; persistence should write targeted concrete `agent.<id>.model` values only for `nexus`, HOW, DO, CHECK, `general`, and `explore`; `build`/`plan`/hidden/custom agents should remain untouched; `setup` remains one command with `--all-model` preferred and `--model` as back-compat alias. Existing worktree has unrelated dirty files (`package.json`, `src/create-config.ts`, `src/plugin/hooks.ts`, `scripts/e2e-subagent-prompts.mjs`, `scripts/e2e-team-policy.mjs`, `.nexus/history.json`) that must be preserved.
tasks.approach: Update `src/cli.ts` to add grouped setup option parsing, targeted write semantics, discovery helpers and interactive grouped flow, plus command help/examples that reflect `--all-model` and grouped flags. Preserve non-interactive scriptability, keep `setup` as the single command, and ensure `--all-model` provides the baseline while specific group flags override it. Update `scripts/e2e-setup-template.mjs` to cover the new help surface and core non-interactive setup semantics. Keep changes minimal and avoid touching unrelated dirty files.
tasks.acceptance:
- `opencode-nexus setup --help` documents grouped setup, `--all-model`, and grouped override flags without regressing command-specific help boundaries
- Non-interactive `setup` supports `--all-model` and grouped model flags, with `--model` preserved as a backward-compatible alias for all-at-once behavior
- Setup writes only surfaced role models (`nexus`, HOW, DO, CHECK, `general`, `explore`) and does not rewrite unrelated/disabled/custom agents by default
- Group-specific flags override the all-at-once baseline deterministically
- Interactive `setup` defaults to grouped flow and exposes a one-shot all-model shortcut; discovery-first logic uses OpenCode CLI with explicit fallback behavior
- Updated setup e2e coverage reflects the new CLI contract and passes
tasks.risk: Highest risk is concentrated in `src/cli.ts` complexity: parsing conflicts, terminal prompting branches, and subprocess discovery behavior can easily regress existing install/update/setup paths. Keep precedence rules simple, scope writes narrowly, and preserve old `--model` compatibility carefully.
